 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3myr","title":"[SC] Attestation + SBOM object schemas","description":"# [SC] Attestation + SBOM object schemas

## Goal
Define canonical schemas and validation rules for SupplyChainAttestation and SBOM objects, including signature requirements and deterministic hashing.

## Scope
- SupplyChainAttestation schema (SLSA/in-toto style provenance) with binding to artifact digest.
- SBOM schema (CycloneDX/SPDX subset) describing components and dependencies.
- Signature requirements and trust-root binding rules.
- Canonical encoding and hash rules (stable across platforms).

## Deliverables
- Schema IDs and versions for:
  - SupplyChainAttestation: subject digest, predicate type, builder identity, buildType, materials, metadata (timestamps), slsa_level, provenance_hash.
  - SBOM: bom_format, bom_version, tool chain, component list (name, version, hashes, licenses), dependency graph.
- Canonical encoding rules (CBOR canonical + JSON canonical) and hashing algorithm selection.
- Validation rules: required fields, allowed predicate types, builder allowlist hooks.

## Tests
- Golden vectors for valid/invalid attestations and SBOMs.
- Deterministic hash tests (same semantic input -> same hash).
- Schema rejection tests for missing/extra fields.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for schema validation and hash determinism checks:
  - {test, schema_id, vector_id, result, duration_ms}.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schemas are versioned, deterministic, and validated by conformance tooling.
- Encoding + hash outputs are stable across runs/OSes.
- Invalid/unsigned objects fail closed with clear error codes.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

Claiming as WindyRaven and mapping existing supply-chain schema code now.","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:13:03Z"},{"id":314,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-3myr","author":"WindyRaven","text":"Implemented supply-chain attestation + SBOM object schemas in fcp-conformance (new JSON schemas + validator functions), added fcp-core supply-chain object types with deterministic canonical JSON/CBOR hashing + strict validation + signature/trust-root rules, and added schema tests/logging coverage. Verification: cargo check -p fcp-core --lib passes via rch exec; workspace cargo check/clippy/fmt and fcp-conformance tests are blocked by pre-existing parse errors in crates/fcp-raptorq/src/golden.rs (invalid literals like 0xMIX_SCAT).","created_at":"2026-03-01T05:25:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3oxr","title":"[IDEA-02.4] RateLimitDeclarations: end-to-end tests","description":"## Goal
Provide end-to-end coverage for rate limit declarations and enforcement.

## Details
- E2E scenario: connector advertises limits, host surfaces them, CLI renders them, and a throttled invoke returns structured retry metadata.
- Logging must include limit pool, remaining, reset timestamp.

## Tests
- E2E script with structured JSONL logs (depends on flywheel_connectors-11g2).
- Unit tests for error mapping and retry hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E runner confirms rate limit metadata from manifest → host → CLI.
- Rate limited invokes include Retry-After and do not execute side effects.","notes":"Updated rate_limit_flow.sh to allow CONNECTOR_ID/OPERATION/ZONE overrides via env. Script still unrun locally (depends on fcp-harness + connector availability).

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-3p99","title":"TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)","description":"# TEST: No-mock coverage + full E2E logging (FCP2)

## Goal
Establish real-component unit/integration coverage (no mocks/fakes) plus a complete E2E script suite with rich structured logging.

## Definition: No mocks/fakes
- No mocking frameworks (wiremock), stub servers, or fake clients in the target suites.
- Tests exercise real code paths (actual protocol parsing, crypto, stores, mesh/session logic, sandbox enforcement).
- For external services (LLM, social APIs), tests must use dedicated sandbox accounts and real HTTP calls OR be marked manual-only with secrets gating.

## Deliverables
- Coverage inventory + gap map.
- Real-component test suites for core crates, mesh, store/repair, host+connector subprocess, sandbox/egress.
- E2E logging schema + validator + expanded scenario runner.

## Non-goals
- Do not delete existing mock-based tests; instead add real-component suites in parallel.
- No reliance on public production accounts.

## Success Criteria
- A coherent dependency graph of tasks that yields: (1) real-component unit/integration coverage, (2) comprehensive E2E scripts with machine-parseable logs.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3pdj","title":"[IDEA-19] Structured Log Redaction + Secret Scanner","description":"## Background
Logging standards forbid secrets/PII, but enforcement is inconsistent. A mechanical scanner reduces risk and enforces compliance across tests and CI artifacts.

## Goals
- Provide a reusable log scanner that detects secrets/PII patterns in JSONL logs.
- Integrate with test harnesses and compliance runner.
- Allow explicit allowlists for safe test fixtures.

## Non-goals
- Replacing runtime redaction; this is a verification layer.

## Approach
- Add a `LogRedactionScanner` in `fcp-testkit` or `fcp-conformance`.
- Detect common secret formats (JWTs, API keys, auth headers, base64 blobs) and obvious PII.
- Expose CLI for scanning artifacts.

## Risks
- False positives; provide explicit per-test allowlists and explainable matches.

## Success Criteria
- CI fails if secrets appear in logs.
- Scanner provides actionable match locations and suggestions.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Scanner detects JWTs, bearer tokens, and API key patterns in JSONL.
- Allowlist requires explicit justification string.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-3pii","title":"TEST: fcp-cbor Unit Tests (Canonical Serialization, SchemaId, Determinism)","description":"# TEST: fcp-cbor Unit Tests (Canonical Serialization, SchemaId, Determinism)

## Goal
Comprehensive test coverage for the fcp-cbor crate ensuring canonical CBOR serialization
is deterministic, schema-aware, and correctly implements RFC 8949 CBOR.

## Test Categories

### 1. Schema Hash Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod schema_hash_tests {
    use fcp_cbor::{SchemaId, SchemaHash};
    use semver::Version;

    #[test]
    fn test_schema_hash_deterministic() {
        let schema1 = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "CapabilityToken", Version::new(2, 0, 0));
        let schema2 = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "CapabilityToken", Version::new(2, 0, 0));

        assert_eq\!(schema1.hash(), schema2.hash());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_schema_hash_different_for_different_versions() {
        let v1 = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "Object", Version::new(1, 0, 0));
        let v2 = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "Object", Version::new(2, 0, 0));

        assert_ne\!(v1.hash(), v2.hash());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_schema_hash_different_for_different_namespaces() {
        let ns1 = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "Message", Version::new(1, 0, 0));
        let ns2 = SchemaId::new("fcp.connector", "Message", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        assert_ne\!(ns1.hash(), ns2.hash());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_schema_hash_format() {
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "ExampleType", Version::new(1, 2, 3));
        let hash = schema.hash();

        // Hash should be 32 bytes (SHA-256)
        assert_eq\!(hash.as_bytes().len(), 32);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_schema_hash_golden_vectors() {
        // Known good hash for specific schema
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.core", "CapabilityObject", Version::new(2, 0, 0));
        let expected = hex::decode("...known_hash...").unwrap();

        assert_eq\!(schema.hash().as_bytes(), expected.as_slice());
    }
}
```

### 2. Canonical Serialization Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod canonical_serialization_tests {
    use fcp_cbor::{CanonicalSerializer, SchemaId, to_canonical_cbor};
    use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    use semver::Version;
    use std::collections::BTreeMap;

    #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Debug)]
    struct TestObject {
        id: u64,
        name: String,
        enabled: bool,
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_canonical_cbor_deterministic() {
        let obj = TestObject {
            id: 42,
            name: "test".to_string(),
            enabled: true,
        };

        let bytes1 = to_canonical_cbor(&obj).unwrap();
        let bytes2 = to_canonical_cbor(&obj).unwrap();

        assert_eq\!(bytes1, bytes2, "Same object must produce identical bytes");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_canonical_cbor_map_key_ordering() {
        // Maps must have keys in lexicographic order (RFC 8949 4.2.1)
        let mut map = BTreeMap::new();
        map.insert("zebra", 1);
        map.insert("apple", 2);
        map.insert("mango", 3);

        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&map).unwrap();

        // Decode and verify key order
        let decoded: ciborium::Value = ciborium::from_reader(&bytes[..]).unwrap();
        if let ciborium::Value::Map(entries) = decoded {
            let keys: Vec<_> = entries.iter()
                .map(|(k, _)| k.as_text().unwrap())
                .collect();
            assert_eq\!(keys, vec\!["apple", "mango", "zebra"]);
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_serializer_includes_schema_prefix() {
        let obj = TestObject { id: 1, name: "test".into(), enabled: true };
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "TestObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        let bytes = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(&obj, &schema).unwrap();

        // First 32 bytes should be schema hash
        let prefix = &bytes[..32];
        assert_eq\!(prefix, schema.hash().as_bytes());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_serializer_roundtrip() {
        let obj = TestObject { id: 123, name: "roundtrip".into(), enabled: false };
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "TestObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        let bytes = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(&obj, &schema).unwrap();
        let decoded: TestObject = CanonicalSerializer::deserialize(&bytes, &schema).unwrap();

        assert_eq\!(obj, decoded);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_deserialize_wrong_schema_fails() {
        let obj = TestObject { id: 1, name: "test".into(), enabled: true };
        let schema_v1 = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "TestObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));
        let schema_v2 = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "TestObject", Version::new(2, 0, 0));

        let bytes = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(&obj, &schema_v1).unwrap();

        // Deserialize with wrong schema should fail
        let result: Result<TestObject, _> = CanonicalSerializer::deserialize(&bytes, &schema_v2);
        assert\!(result.is_err());
    }
}
```

### 3. Integer Encoding Tests (RFC 8949)

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod integer_encoding_tests {
    use fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor;

    #[test]
    fn test_smallest_integer_encoding() {
        // RFC 8949 requires smallest possible encoding
        // 0-23: single byte
        // 24-255: two bytes
        // 256-65535: three bytes
        // etc.

        let small = 23u64;
        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&small).unwrap();
        assert_eq\!(bytes.len(), 1); // 0x17

        let medium = 24u64;
        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&medium).unwrap();
        assert_eq\!(bytes.len(), 2); // 0x18 0x18

        let large = 256u64;
        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&large).unwrap();
        assert_eq\!(bytes.len(), 3); // 0x19 0x01 0x00
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_negative_integer_encoding() {
        let neg = -1i64;
        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&neg).unwrap();
        assert_eq\!(bytes, vec\![0x20]); // -1 encoded as major type 1
    }
}
```

### 4. Floating Point Handling Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod float_tests {
    use fcp_cbor::to_canonical_cbor;

    #[test]
    fn test_float_canonical_form() {
        // Floats that can be represented as integers should use integer form
        let whole = 42.0f64;
        let bytes = to_canonical_cbor(&whole).unwrap();
        // Should be encoded as integer 42, not float
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_nan_handling() {
        let nan = f64::NAN;
        // NaN should either:
        // - Fail serialization, OR
        // - Use canonical quiet NaN encoding
        let result = to_canonical_cbor(&nan);
        // Document expected behavior
    }
}
```

### 5. Content Hash (ObjectId) Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod object_id_tests {
    use fcp_cbor::{CanonicalSerializer, SchemaId, ObjectId};
    use serde::Serialize;
    use semver::Version;

    #[derive(Serialize)]
    struct DataObject {
        value: u64,
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_object_id_deterministic() {
        let obj = DataObject { value: 42 };
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "DataObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        let id1 = ObjectId::compute(&obj, &schema).unwrap();
        let id2 = ObjectId::compute(&obj, &schema).unwrap();

        assert_eq\!(id1, id2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_object_id_different_for_different_content() {
        let obj1 = DataObject { value: 1 };
        let obj2 = DataObject { value: 2 };
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "DataObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        let id1 = ObjectId::compute(&obj1, &schema).unwrap();
        let id2 = ObjectId::compute(&obj2, &schema).unwrap();

        assert_ne\!(id1, id2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_object_id_blake3_based() {
        let obj = DataObject { value: 123 };
        let schema = SchemaId::new("fcp.test", "DataObject", Version::new(1, 0, 0));

        let bytes = CanonicalSerializer::serialize(&obj, &schema).unwrap();
        let expected_hash = blake3::hash(&bytes);

        let object_id = ObjectId::compute(&obj, &schema).unwrap();
        assert_eq\!(object_id.as_bytes(), expected_hash.as_bytes());
    }
}
```

## Golden Vectors

Store reference test data in `crates/fcp-cbor/tests/vectors/`:

- `schema_hashes.json` - Known schema ID to hash mappings
- `canonical_encoding.cbor` - Reference encodings for various types
- `integer_encoding.json` - Integer encoding test cases per RFC 8949
- `map_ordering.json` - Map key ordering test cases

## Logging Requirements (per 1n78.35)

All test runs MUST emit structured JSONL:

```json
{"ts":"2026-01-28T12:00:00.000Z","level":"DEBUG","component":"cbor.test","event":"serialize","schema":"fcp.test/TestObject@1.0.0","bytes":156}
{"ts":"2026-01-28T12:00:00.001Z","level":"DEBUG","component":"cbor.test","event":"schema_hash","namespace":"fcp.core","type":"CapabilityObject","version":"2.0.0","hash":"sha256:abc123"}
{"ts":"2026-01-28T12:00:00.002Z","level":"INFO","component":"cbor.test","event":"roundtrip","result":"pass","duration_ns":5000}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All unit tests pass with `cargo test --package fcp-cbor`
- [ ] Canonical encoding is deterministic (fuzz tested)
- [ ] Map keys are lexicographically ordered (RFC 8949 compliant)
- [ ] Schema hash is stable across runs and versions
- [ ] ObjectId derivation matches reference implementation
- [ ] Integer encoding uses smallest form (RFC 8949 4.2.1)
